seoTitle: How to Copy and Paste a Table Into Canva
metaDescription: Bring a table from another app into Canva and format it cleanly for presentations, reports, and marketing materials.
intro: Rebuilding a table from scratch in a design tool is tedious, so being able to copy one in from another application saves real time. This guide covers the steps for bringing a table into Canva and cleaning up the formatting once it's there. It's useful for marketers and business professionals who need to present data clearly.

steps.heading: Select the table
steps.body: In your source application, select the table you want to bring into Canva.

steps.heading: Copy the table
steps.body: Copy the selected table data using your standard copy command.

steps.heading: Paste into Canva
steps.body: Paste the table into your Canva design where you want it to appear.

steps.heading: Clean up formatting
steps.body: Adjust fonts, spacing, and colors so the pasted table matches the rest of your design.

faq.question: Does pasting a table into Canva preserve the original formatting?
faq.answer: Some formatting may carry over, but you'll likely need to adjust fonts and spacing to match your Canva design.

faq.question: Can I edit table data after pasting it into Canva?
faq.answer: Yes, once pasted you can edit the text and styling like any other Canva element.

faq.question: What table formats work best for pasting into Canva?
faq.answer: Simpler tables tend to paste more cleanly than heavily formatted spreadsheets with merged cells or complex styling.
